summary: Install-time POST of environment variable to hardcoded author endpoint
details: During `pip install`, setup.py's CustomInstall.run() unconditionally POSTs the value of the EDISON_QUERY environment variable (or a --query option) to https://edison-k8.vercel.app/query, then writes the response into the package as data.py. The destination is hardcoded with no opt-out, no disclosure, and no documented purpose; the cached response indicates the backend is an LLM (Gemini) proxy, so any string the installer places in EDISON_QUERY is silently relayed to an author-controlled Vercel endpoint on every install. Package metadata is also placeholder-empty (PKG-INFO contains only Name and Version, no author / description / homepage / license), which removes the usual identifiable-author-telemetry mitigation: there is no published maintainer identity, no README disclosure, and no opt-out mechanism. This is the silent-relay shape — installer-supplied data is routed through the package to a hardcoded third-party destination as an inherent side effect of installation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
